body.title: Comms module: channel routing, envelopes, transports, logging
body.result: CommsModule rack shelf: ChannelMessage envelope, Channel definitions, send/read/subscribe routing, MemoryTransport + PostgresTransport, conversation file logging. 35 tests.
body.description:
Build the comms rack module for the utility closet — Lt Uhura's console.

## Core
- ChannelMessage envelope: id, channel (comms://), source, timestamp, content_type, payload, reply_to, metadata, retention
- Channel definition: address, direction (R/W/RW), delivery (pull/push), notify, transport, retention, log_path, created/last_active
- CommsModule API: register_channel, send, read, subscribe, unsubscribe, list_channels, channel_status
- Channel persistence: definitions in Postgres (comms_channels table), conversation history in log files (~/.TheIgors/logs/comms/<channel>.conversation.log)
- Retention tiers: forever (relationships), 1y (default), 30d (operational), ephemeral (debug)
- Log files are JSON-per-line, human-readable and parseable. The log IS the persistence; Postgres indexes for fast search.

## Auto-creation
- On MCP/agent connect: check if comms://<agent-id> existed before → reconnect to same log + config. If new → create channel + log file.
- Log path deterministic from channel address: comms://discord/dm-akien → discord--dm-akien.conversation.log

## Notification dispatch
- Per-channel notify flag + per-subscriber checkbox
- MCP subscribers (CC, Igor) → queue in MCP inbox
- WebSocket subscribers (browser) → push via WS, tab color change
- TMUX subscribers → inject into target pane

## Transports (pluggable backends)
- websocket — Web UI channels (push, read/write)
- mcp — CC hook reads, Igor channel_read (pull, read/write)
- tmux — inject text into terminal pane (push, write-only)
- file — log file output (pull, write-only)
- discord — Discord bot bridge (push, read/write)
- postgres — persistent history index (pull, read/write)

## IgorBase integration
- channel_log() method routes through comms instead of direct file write
- Replaces scattered logger.info() with channel-routed logging

## Files
- lab/utility_closet/comms.py — CommsModule, Channel, ChannelMessage
- lab/utility_closet/transports/ — one file per transport
- tests/test_comms.py — unit tests with mock transports
- UC server wires CommsModule on startup, registers default channels (Shared, per-agent)

## Design for (but don't build yet)
- comms://model/* channels for inference routing (T-uc-inference-channel)
- reply_to correlation ID in envelope supports request/response patterns
- content_type distinguishes chat/log/inference-request/inference-response

## Depends on
- T-uc-rack-architecture (module registration interface)

## Related
- T-uc-channel-tabs-redesign — web UI consumes comms channels
- T-uc-named-channels (#403) — predecessor, subsumed by this
- T-igor-mcp-cc-bidirectional (#461) — notification delivery uses comms
